description: CycleBench medical foundation: guideline-seeded associations strengthened by female-specialized dataset evidence. Not a diagnostic system.

entities.label: PCOS discussion cluster
entities.description: Irregular cycles + androgenic / metabolic cues clinicians may explore. Not a diagnosis.
entities.notes: Irregular cycles + androgenic / metabolic cues clinicians may explore. Not a diagnosis.

entities.label: Endometriosis-symptom discussion cluster
entities.description: Cyclical pelvic pain patterns clinicians may explore. Evidence-light in this graph; not a diagnosis.
entities.notes: Cyclical pelvic pain patterns clinicians may explore. Evidence-light in this graph; not a diagnosis.

entities.label: Menopause-transition discussion cluster
entities.description: Midlife cycle change + vasomotor cues. Stage category talk only — not a home diagnosis.
entities.notes: Midlife cycle change + vasomotor cues. Stage category talk only — not a home diagnosis.

entities.label: Thyroid discussion cluster
entities.description: Fatigue/mood/cycle change with thyroid labs context. Not a diagnosis.
entities.notes: Fatigue/mood/cycle change with thyroid labs context. Not a diagnosis.

entities.label: Anemia / heavy-bleed discussion cluster
entities.description: Heavy bleeding + fatigue cue. Not a diagnosis.
entities.notes: Heavy bleeding + fatigue cue. Not a diagnosis.

entities.label: Premenstrual mood discussion cluster
entities.description: Luteal-window mood changes clinicians may explore. Not a diagnosis.
entities.notes: Luteal-window mood changes clinicians may explore. Not a diagnosis.

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Headaches that cluster in the menstrual window are a commonly recognized timing pattern.
associations.ask_doctor: My headaches seem tied to my period — does that timing matter?
associations.title: Headaches near menses

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Some people notice migraine load rise in the late luteal / premenstrual window.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my migraine days line up with the second half of my cycle?
associations.title: Migraine in the luteal window

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Cramps commonly concentrate in the menstrual phase; severity and sudden change still matter clinically.
associations.ask_doctor: How heavy or disruptive are my cramps across cycles?
associations.title: Cramps and menses

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Mood shifts in the luteal window are commonly discussed; tracking helps separate cycle-linked from constant patterns.
associations.ask_doctor: Are my mood changes mainly in the week before my period?
associations.title: Mood changes before the period

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Bloating often co-travels with the late cycle; diet and GI causes still matter.
associations.ask_doctor: Is my bloating mostly cyclical?
associations.title: Bloating around the luteal window

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Cyclical breast tenderness is frequently luteal-predominant.
associations.ask_doctor: Is breast tenderness a usual part of my cycle or new?
associations.title: Breast tenderness and the luteal phase

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Fatigue can rise around menses but often has multiple contributors (sleep, iron, mood).
associations.ask_doctor: Is my fatigue worse on period days, or all month?
associations.title: Fatigue around menses

associations.caveats: Timing associations are not diagnoses; many non-cycle causes exist.
associations.source: Menstrual physiology / clinical gynecology & headache medicine (textbook-level timing associations).
associations.talking_point: Pelvic pain that tracks menses is a pattern clinicians often explore further when severe or progressive.
associations.ask_doctor: Is my pelvic pain mainly with periods, and is it getting worse over time?
associations.title: Pelvic pain timed to menses

associations.caveats: Counseling relevance only — not a home diagnosis or instruction to stop medication.
associations.source: UKMEC / CDC MEC style guidance: combined hormonal contraception and migraine (esp. with aura) is a counseling point.
associations.talking_point: Headaches that started or changed around a combined (estrogen-containing) contraceptive are a pattern clinicians often review. This does not mean the method caused the headaches — it means the combination is worth discussing, especially if headaches come with aura (flashing lights, zigzag vision).
associations.ask_doctor: Do my headaches change with my contraceptive method, and should we review whether an estrogen-containing option is still a good fit?
associations.title: Hormonal contraception and headaches

associations.caveats: User may not know what aura is; ask, don't assume.
associations.source: UKMEC / CDC MEC: migraine with aura is a key counseling modifier for combined hormonal contraception.
associations.talking_point: If headaches include aura, clinicians usually want that detail when reviewing estrogen-containing contraception. Aura presence changes the counseling conversation.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my headaches include aura, and does that change contraceptive choices for me?
associations.title: Aura and combined contraception

associations.source: Contraceptive counseling: bleeding-pattern changes are common after method start/switch.
associations.talking_point: Spotting or bleeding-pattern changes after starting or switching a ring/patch are commonly discussed in the adjustment window.
associations.ask_doctor: Is this bleeding pattern expected for my method, and when should we reassess?
associations.title: Bleeding pattern and ring/patch

associations.source: Contraceptive counseling: bleeding-pattern changes are common after method start/switch.
associations.talking_point: Spotting or bleeding-pattern changes after starting or switching a progestogen-only pill are commonly discussed in the adjustment window.
associations.ask_doctor: Is this bleeding pattern expected for my method, and when should we reassess?
associations.title: Bleeding pattern and progestogen-only pill

associations.source: Contraceptive counseling: bleeding-pattern changes are common after method start/switch.
associations.talking_point: Spotting or bleeding-pattern changes after starting or switching a hormonal IUD are commonly discussed in the adjustment window.
associations.ask_doctor: Is this bleeding pattern expected for my method, and when should we reassess?
associations.title: Bleeding pattern and hormonal IUD

associations.source: General contraceptive counseling: adjustment windows after start/stop/switch.
associations.talking_point: When contraception is started, stopped, or switched, the body often needs weeks to months to settle. New or changing symptoms in that window are worth noting on a timeline — not as proof of cause, but as useful context.
associations.ask_doctor: These symptoms showed up around my contraception change — is that a plausible association, or should we look elsewhere?
associations.title: Symptoms after a contraception change

associations.source: Headache and sleep medicine: bidirectional association between sleep disruption and headache.
associations.talking_point: Poor sleep and headaches often travel together. Sleep disturbance can worsen headache load, and headaches can disrupt sleep — so clinicians usually ask about both rather than treating them as separate mysteries.
associations.ask_doctor: I've had rough sleep alongside the headaches — should we address sleep as part of this picture?
associations.title: Sleep and headache overlap

associations.source: Sleep and mood are tightly linked in clinical assessment.
associations.talking_point: Mood changes alongside poor sleep are a common co-travel pattern clinicians unpack together.
associations.ask_doctor: Could sleep be contributing to how I've been feeling emotionally?
associations.title: Sleep and mood

associations.source: Clinical basics: sleep debt is a primary fatigue contributor.
associations.talking_point: Fatigue with poor sleep often improves when sleep is addressed — but persistent fatigue still deserves a broader look.
associations.ask_doctor: How much of my fatigue lines up with nights of bad sleep?
associations.title: Sleep and fatigue

associations.source: Behavioral medicine: stress and mood covary strongly.
associations.talking_point: High stress and mood changes commonly overlap; both deserve space in the story you bring to a clinician.
associations.ask_doctor: How much stress has been in the background of these mood changes?
associations.title: Stress and mood

associations.caveats: Vasomotor symptoms have non-menopausal causes too.
associations.source: Menopause practice guidelines: vasomotor symptoms are cardinal midlife counseling topics.
associations.talking_point: In the 40s–50s, hot flashes are commonly discussed as part of the menopause transition. Timing and severity help a clinician place you on that spectrum — without a home diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: Given my age and hot flashes, what would you track next?
associations.title: Hot flashes in midlife context

associations.source: Menopause practice guidelines: night sweats as vasomotor counseling topic.
associations.talking_point: Night sweats in midlife are often reviewed alongside cycle changes as a menopause-transition cue.
associations.ask_doctor: Are my night sweats likely part of a midlife transition pattern?
associations.title: Night sweats in midlife context

associations.source: STRAW+10 / menopause staging frameworks: cycle irregularity is a transition marker.
associations.talking_point: Changing cycle regularity in the 40s–50s is a classic counseling marker of the menopause transition.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my cycle changes fit a menopause-transition pattern for my age?
associations.title: Cycle irregularity in midlife

associations.source: Gynecology basics: secondary amenorrhea (≈3+ months) warrants clinical evaluation.
associations.talking_point: Going three or more months without a period (when pregnancy is not the explanation) is something clinicians usually want to know about. There are many possible reasons; the useful move is a proper history and, if needed, labs — not guessing at home.
associations.ask_doctor: I haven't had a period in a while — what should we check, and how urgently?
associations.title: Long gap since last period

associations.source: Reproductive endocrinology: FSH rises across the menopause transition (context-dependent).
associations.talking_point: FSH can help contextualize midlife stage in clinic; a single value is not a home diagnosis of menopause.
associations.ask_doctor: Would FSH or other labs add useful context for my stage?
associations.title: FSH as a midlife marker

associations.source: Reproductive endocrinology: estradiol declines across the transition (variable).
associations.talking_point: Estradiol levels can inform midlife assessment but vary widely cycle-to-cycle before menopause.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my estradiol results mean anything for symptoms I'm having?
associations.title: Estradiol in midlife context

associations.caveats: May be trained on synthetic SWAN-like data until real SWAN is loaded.
associations.source: CycleBench menopause-stage model (research estimate).
associations.talking_point: A research model can estimate a stage category from age/symptoms/labs — association only, not a clinical diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: How should we interpret this stage estimate alongside my history?
associations.title: Menopause-stage model signal

associations.caveats: Short histories may pad a single day ×5; labels are research estimates.
associations.source: CycleBench personalized phase model (FedPer GRU on mcPHASES).
associations.talking_point: A research sequence model can estimate menstrual phase from daily symptoms and wearable-style channels — association only, not a clinical diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: How should we interpret this cycle-phase estimate alongside my history?
associations.title: Cycle-phase model signal

associations.source: Reproductive endocrinology: oligomenorrhea is a core PCOS counseling cue (many causes).
associations.talking_point: Irregular cycles plus symptoms like weight change, excess hair growth, acne, or pelvic discomfort are a cluster clinicians may explore further (including hormonal workup). Many things can cause irregular cycles — this is a reason for a conversation, not a label.
associations.ask_doctor: My cycles are irregular and I've also noticed other changes — what evaluations would you consider?
associations.title: Irregular cycles and related symptoms

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: AMH is sometimes discussed in PCOS workups; elevated values are contextual, not diagnostic alone.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: AMH in PCOS-cluster context

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: LH (and LH/FSH context) may appear in hormonal panels when irregular cycles are evaluated.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: LH in PCOS-cluster context

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: Excess hair growth with irregular cycles is a hyperandrogenic cue clinicians may explore — many causes exist.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: Excess hair growth cue

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: Acne plus irregular cycles can be part of a broader hormonal conversation.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: Acne with irregular cycles

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: Weight change alongside irregular cycles is useful context, not a diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: Weight change with irregular cycles

associations.source: PCOS clinical frameworks (Rotterdam-style counseling cues) — discussion only.
associations.talking_point: Ultrasound follicle counts are used in clinical PCOS criteria frameworks — imaging belongs in clinic, not apps.
associations.ask_doctor: Does this fit a hormonal pattern worth testing?
associations.title: Follicle count context

associations.caveats: Single-region, cross-sectional, not externally validated as a device.
associations.source: CycleBench PCOS-risk model trained on Kaggle Kerala clinic cohort (n=541).
associations.talking_point: A research model can estimate how similar a feature pattern is to a labeled PCOS clinic cohort. That is a statistical association — not a diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: How should we use this screening-style signal in my clinical workup?
associations.title: PCOS-risk model signal

associations.caveats: Evidence-light edge in this foundation; do not over-call.
associations.source: Gynecology: severe cyclical pelvic pain is a reason to evaluate; many etiologies.
associations.talking_point: Severe pelvic pain that tracks the cycle is something clinicians take seriously. Endometriosis is one of several possibilities — this graph marks it as a low-prior discussion cluster, not a conclusion.
associations.ask_doctor: My pelvic pain is severe and cyclical — what should we evaluate?
associations.title: Cyclical pelvic pain — discussion cue

associations.source: Endocrinology basics: thyroid labs are often considered in unexplained fatigue.
associations.talking_point: Persistent fatigue sometimes prompts thyroid testing; abnormal TSH needs clinical interpretation.
associations.ask_doctor: Would thyroid labs be reasonable given my fatigue?
associations.title: Thyroid labs and fatigue

associations.source: Thyroid evaluation frameworks.
associations.talking_point: TSH is a common entry lab when thyroid contribution to symptoms is considered.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my thyroid labs explain any of these symptoms?
associations.title: TSH in thyroid discussion cluster

associations.source: Metabolic–reproductive overlap counseling (insulin resistance context).
associations.talking_point: Glucose/metabolic labs sometimes sit alongside reproductive evaluations; they do not label a syndrome alone.
associations.ask_doctor: Should we look at metabolic labs as part of this picture?
associations.title: Glucose context with reproductive symptoms

associations.source: Gynecology / hematology: heavy menses + fatigue prompts iron evaluation discussion.
associations.talking_point: Heavy periods with fatigue are a classic reason clinicians check iron status — many other causes of fatigue exist.
associations.ask_doctor: Could heavy bleeding be contributing to my fatigue, and should we check iron?
associations.title: Heavy bleeding and fatigue

associations.source: Premenstrual disorders clinical frameworks (discussion only).
associations.talking_point: Mood symptoms that reliably appear in the luteal window and ease after menses start are a pattern clinicians may explore.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my mood symptoms clear after my period starts?
associations.title: Premenstrual mood pattern

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported headaches may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: headaches

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported cramps may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: cramps

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported fatigue may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: fatigue

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported moodswing may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: moodswing

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported bloating may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: bloating

associations.source: mcPHASES aggregate symptom×phase rates (PhysioNet; aggregate only).
associations.talking_point: In longitudinal cohorts, reported sorebreasts may concentrate in particular cycle phases.
associations.ask_doctor: Does my pattern match what shows up in cycle-phase research cohorts?
associations.title: Cohort enrichment: sorebreasts

associations.source: Menopause practice guidelines: vasomotor symptoms + cycle change are cardinal midlife counseling topics.
associations.talking_point: In the 40s–50s, changing cycle regularity, hot flashes, and night sweats are commonly discussed as part of the menopause transition. Timing and severity help a clinician place you on that spectrum — without a home diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: Given my age and these changes, does this sound like a menopause-transition pattern, and what would you track next?
associations.title: Midlife cycle and vasomotor changes

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Nausea can accompany menstrual days for some people; severe or persistent nausea still deserves a broad look.
associations.ask_doctor: Is my nausea mainly on period days?
associations.title: Nausea around menses

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Some people notice fogginess in the late cycle; sleep and stress often co-travel.
associations.ask_doctor: Does brain fog track a particular part of my cycle?
associations.title: Brain fog and cycle timing

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Desire can vary across the cycle; sudden persistent loss is still worth clinical mention.
associations.ask_doctor: Has my libido changed recently or only in one cycle window?
associations.title: Libido and cycle window

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Progestogen-only pills commonly change bleeding patterns, especially early on.
associations.ask_doctor: Is this bleeding pattern expected on my progestogen-only pill?
associations.title: POP and bleeding pattern

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Implants often change bleeding — from amenorrhea to irregular spotting.
associations.ask_doctor: Is my implant-related bleeding within the expected range?
associations.title: Implant and bleeding pattern

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Copper IUDs are classically associated with heavier menstrual bleeding for some users.
associations.ask_doctor: Could my copper IUD be contributing to heavier periods?
associations.title: Copper IUD and heavier flow

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Menopausal hormone therapy is discussed clinically for vasomotor symptoms — decisions belong with a clinician.
associations.ask_doctor: Is menopausal hormone therapy something we should discuss for these symptoms?
associations.title: MHT and vasomotor symptoms

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Stress and headache commonly intensify together; both belong in the history.
associations.ask_doctor: How much stress has been sitting alongside these headaches?
associations.title: Stress and headache

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Sudden intense exercise or energy deficit can associate with cycle changes in some people.
associations.ask_doctor: Did my cycles change after a big shift in training or fueling?
associations.title: Exercise change and cycles

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: New medications can temporally overlap new symptoms — worth a timeline, not an assumption of cause.
associations.ask_doctor: Did symptoms start around a medication change?
associations.title: Medication change window

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Prolactin is sometimes checked when periods stop; interpretation is clinical.
associations.ask_doctor: Should prolactin be part of evaluating my missing periods?
associations.title: Prolactin in amenorrhea workups

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: SHBG can appear in hormonal panels when androgen excess is being explored.
associations.ask_doctor: Do my androgen-related labs include SHBG, and what do they mean together?
associations.title: SHBG in androgen/PCOS-cluster labs

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Insulin/metabolic labs sometimes accompany reproductive evaluations.
associations.ask_doctor: Should insulin or metabolic labs be part of this workup?
associations.title: Insulin context with PCOS-cluster cues

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: HbA1c provides longer-window glucose context; not a reproductive diagnosis.
associations.ask_doctor: Would an HbA1c help round out the metabolic side of this picture?
associations.title: HbA1c as metabolic context

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: The postpartum period is a high-attention window for mood changes — seek clinical care promptly for concerning symptoms.
associations.ask_doctor: Are these mood changes in a postpartum window, and how urgently should we review them?
associations.title: Postpartum mood window

associations.source: Expanded CycleBench foundation seed (guideline/textbook-level associations).
associations.talking_point: Cycles can be irregular for years after menarche; persistent severe symptoms still deserve care.
associations.ask_doctor: Is this irregularity expected for my age since periods started?
associations.title: Adolescent cycle irregularity

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported headaches clustered most in the menstrual phase (χ²=18.89, p=0.00029).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported cramps clustered most in the menstrual phase (χ²=203.9, p=0.0).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported fatigue did not show a clear phase cluster (p=0.12057).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported moodswing clustered most in the menstrual phase (χ²=8.45, p=0.03749).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported bloating clustered most in the menstrual phase (χ²=32.97, p=0.0).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: In the mcPHASES cohort (n=42), reported sorebreasts clustered most in the menstrual phase (χ²=52.82, p=0.0).
evidence.license_note: PhysioNet restricted — aggregate statistics only; no participant rows.

evidence.summary_sentence: NHANES female reference (age band 38-47, n=581): fsh median 6.82 mIU/mL (2.5th–97.5th percentile 1.395–89.495). Cross-sectional population ranges — not personal targets.
evidence.license_note: NHANES public domain; harmonized export CC-BY-4.0.

evidence.summary_sentence: NHANES female reference (age band 38-47, n=567): estradiol median 79.3 pg/mL (2.5th–97.5th percentile 2.801–409.0). Cross-sectional population ranges — not personal targets.
evidence.license_note: NHANES public domain; harmonized export CC-BY-4.0.

evidence.summary_sentence: A CycleBench PCOS-risk model is available (trained on 541 Kerala clinic patients). Held-out balanced accuracy ≈ 0.8646. Research screening signal only — not a diagnosis.
evidence.license_note: Model checkpoint derived from Kaggle PCOS dataset (not redistributed).

evidence.summary_sentence: A CycleBench menopause-stage model is available for midlife category estimates. Training data source: synthetic_swan_like. Stage category estimate only — not a diagnosis.
evidence.license_note: If synthetic_swan_like, metrics are illustrative until real SWAN is loaded.

evidence.summary_sentence: Related NIH-sourced Q&A topic on file: “Is Basilar migraine inherited ?” (citation for phrasing context only).
evidence.license_note: MedQuAD from NIH website-derived QA; citation only.
